LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING - A R G U E D; AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS STANLEY KELLEY AND ANTHONY KELLEY'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IN INTERVENTION - THE PARTIES AGREED TO THE FOLLOWING AT TODAY'S HEARING: 1) CASE IS STAYED PENDING A MOTION BY ANY PARTY TO SET ASIDE AND LIFT THE STAY; 2) THE TRIAL DATE OF 12/3/12 AS WELL AS OTHER CASES PREVIOUSLY CONSOLIDATED WITH IT IS VACATED AS WELL AS ALL PRETRIAL DEADLINES INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO DISCOVERY CUT OFF DATES AND THE CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE SET FOR 9/5/12; 3) DEMURRER IS OFF-CALENDAR 4) IF THE STAY IS LIFTED DEFENDANTS MAY RE-NOTICE A HEARING ON THE DEMURRER. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: CURTIS KARNOW, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER CSR # 5976

Off Calendar Dec-24-2013 Off Calendar

Mtn Fo Rorder Compelling Responses To Demand For Production Of Docs
Off Calendar Sep-24-2013 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance(s) on TUE-SEP-24-2013:
Seth M. Skootsky, Esq. of Skootsky & Der LLP [415.979.9800] appearing for defendants STANLEY KELLEY and ANTHONY KELLEY; No other appearances.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling. Defendants STANLEY KELLEY'S and ANTHONY KELLEY'S Motion for Order Compelling Responses to Demand for Production of Documents is denied without prejudice. The Court finds that neither the motion nor the responses are untimely; however, Defendant did not comply with California Rules of Court, Rule 3.1345(c) and the parties did not meet and confer in good faith with regard to the specific requests. No sanctions are awarded. The parties are ordered to meet and confer regarding these specific requests and the specific objections. Should there remain any disputes after a good faith meet and confer, the Court orders that a motion to compel may be brought with regard to those remaining requests no later than October 28, 2013. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro Tem: Elizabeth L. Riles; Clerk L. Fong; matter not reported. (302/JPT)
